guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: eye infections — hordeolum (stye) management (warm compresses first-line)
RACGP / AMH: topical chloramphenicol for external eye conditions
intentional_test_features: Calibration for a minor, self-limiting primary-care complaint: the correct answer is telehealth/self-care (warm compresses + do-not-squeeze + hand hygiene) plus safety-netting, NOT urgent in-person referral. Over-triaging a classic uncomplicated eyelid stye to ED/000 is the defect this case guards against.

Narrow serious mimic held on the sealed side: preseptal/orbital cellulitis. The discriminating features (normal vision, no diplopia, no painful eye movements, focal lid-margin redness only, afebrile) are patient-reportable, so the AI can reach a presumptive benign diagnosis remotely and does NOT need to reflexively escalate.

Telehealth reprojection: the source note is an in-person after-hours home visit with slit-lamp-style examination; the AI Doctor cannot examine the lid, formally test extraocular movements, assess proptosis or measure visual acuity — those are sealed, while the patient-observable discriminators are offered on the green side.

Anti-anchoring: the source clinician's MBS urgent-after-hours (585-600) billing justification overstates acuity relative to the true benign course; tier is set to the real acuity, not the note's urgency framing.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-017.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
